title: aster asperulus
intro: Aster asperulus, also known as the rough aster, is a perennial plant in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Aster asperulus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
